_scoring_notes.kinetic_formula: 40% intercept rate (scaled 90-100% → 0-100) + 20% absolute debris penalty (100 - debris*8) + 20% day-over-day trend (100 - delta*10) + 20% 7-day avg trend (100 - delta*10)
_scoring_notes.social_formula: Base 100. Deductions: -5 per NCEMA alert in last 24h, -5 US embassy partial advisory, -5 AUH partial restriction, -10 per full embassy departure advisory, -10 per airport closure

dailyBriefing: On March 26, UAE air defences successfully intercepted a ballistic missile targeting Abu Dhabi, with falling debris in Sweihan resulting in two fatalities and three injuries. While the intercept rate remains at 100%, the diplomatic situation is critical following the expiration of the Hormuz ultimatum and upgraded travel advisories from the UK and US. Infrastructure remains operational with supermarkets utilizing air freight to maintain stock levels, though overall social tension is elevated due to ongoing alerts and reduced airport capacity.

schoolClosures.label: Distance Learning Only
schoolClosures.guidance: KHDA has extended distance learning for all schools through April 3.

supermarketStock.label: Adequate — Air Freight Supplement
supermarketStock.guidance: Major chains remain stocked via air freight. Prices rising on some imported goods.

fuelAvailability.label: Normal Supply
fuelAvailability.guidance: ENOC and ADNOC stations fully operational across Dubai.
